name: Barbell Bent-Over Row
form_cues: Hips back, torso roughly 45° — flat back
Pull bar to lower chest / upper abs, drive elbows back
Squeeze shoulder blades together at the top, control the eccentric
Don't shrug or yank with biceps
notes: Compound back movement. Complements lat pulldown (vertical pull) with horizontal pull. Use lifting straps if grip fails before back.
video_id_query: Barbell bent over row proper form Jeff Nippard
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width, barbell over mid-foot. Grip slightly wider than shoulder width, pronated.
Hinge at hips, push hips back, keep slight knee bend. Torso roughly 45° to the floor, back flat.
Brace core. From this hinged position, pull the bar to your lower chest / upper abs by driving the elbows back.
Squeeze shoulder blades together at the top. Pause briefly.
Lower under control. Don't let the torso pop up to swing the weight.
